items.title: What does mental health case management do?

expanded_element.title: What Do Behavioral Health Case Managers Do: Daily Work & Skills
expanded_element.description: Behavioral Health Case Managers assess and treat individuals with mental, emotional, or substance abuse problems, including abuse of alcohol, tobacco, and/or other drugs. Activities may include individual and group therapy, crisis intervention, case management, client advocacy, prevention, and education.

items.title: What is the 3 month rule in mental health?

expanded_element.title: How the Three-Month Rule Pertains to Mental Health - Simmrin Law Group
expanded_element.description: A healthcare provider may apply the “three-month rule” regarding a patient's well-being. In this instance, a patient can be forced to enter a psychiatric hospital. From here, hospital staff may try to keep the patient against their will for up to three months before they consider letting the patient leave.

items.title: What is Tim's Law in Kentucky?

expanded_element.title: Casey's Law / Tim's Law | LouisvilleKY.gov
expanded_element.description: Assisted Outpatient Treatment (AOT), also known as Tim's Law, is a court-ordered program designed to ensure that individuals with serious mental illness receive continuous treatment while living in the community.

items.title: What are some examples of severe mental health conditions?

expanded_element.title: Serious Mental Illness - Veterans Affairs
expanded_element.description: SMI includes major depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, obsessive compulsive disorder (OCD), panic disorder, post traumatic stress (PTSD) and borderline personality disorder (VA).
